title: saxifraga oppositifolia subsp. smalliana
intro: Saxifraga oppositifolia subsp. smalliana, also known as small-flowered saxifrage, is a plant species in the saxifrage family that is native to the Arctic tundra. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other minor ailments. The health benefits of Saxifraga oppositifolia subsp. smalliana include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing process, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Saxifraga oppositifolia subsp. smalliana typically involve infusions, tinctures, and salves made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be applied topically to affected areas.
